title: ValueTask<T>: когда брать и его ограничения

cards.prompt: A ValueTask wrapping a ready value completes synchronously (IsCompleted True) with no per-call Task heap allocation
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; ValueTask<int> Cached(int x) => new ValueTask<int>(x); int r = await Cached(7); Console.WriteLine($"r={r} completed={Cached(7).IsCompleted}");

cards.prompt: default(ValueTask<int>) is a synchronously-completed result whose value is default(TResult) == 0
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; var d = default(ValueTask<int>); Console.WriteLine($"{d.IsCompletedSuccessfully} {d.Result}");

cards.prompt: ValueTask<int> is a value type (True) of 4 instance fields; Task<int> is a reference type (False) of one reference field
cards.code: using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using System.Reflection; var t = typeof(ValueTask<int>); int fields = t.GetFields(BindingFlags.Instance|BindingFlags.NonPublic|BindingFlags.Public).Length; Console.WriteLine($"{t.IsValueType} {typeof(Task<int>).IsValueType} fields={fields}");
